LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 3, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR #11604
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-01-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT PRESENTED A LEGITIMATE REASON (I.E. THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PASSED THE PHYSICAL AGILITY TEST ("PAT") FOR NOT PROMOTING THE PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE THAT THAT THIS REASON WAS PRETEXTUAL. FOR THE PURPOSES OF THIS MOTION, PLAINTIFF STIPULATES THAT HE HAS NOT NEEDED AN ACCOMMODATION SINCE AUGUST 2007. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE IS THAT ANY HARASSMENT OF PLAINTIFF WAS NEITHER SEVERE NOR PERVASIVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-17-2012 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-16-2012 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-01-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2012 Off Calendar

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Larry Hecimovich, Esq., DCA City and County of SF, 415-554-3933, for Defendant and moving party
Roderick P. Bushnell, Esq., Law Offices of Bushnell, Caplan, Fielding & Maier, LLP, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment. Granted. Defendant presented a legitimate reason (i.e. that plaintiff has not passed the Physical Agility Test ("PAT") for not promoting the plaintiff. Plaintiff has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue that that this reason was pretextual. For the purposes of this motion, Plaintiff stipulates that he has not needed an accommodation since August 2007. The undisputed evidence is that any harassment of plaintiff was neither severe nor pervasive.
Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Feb-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry Hecimovich, Esq., DCA City and County of SF, 415-554-3933, for Defendant and moving party
Roderick P. Bushnell, Esq., Law Offices of Bushnell, Caplan, Fielding & Maier, LLP, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff. Denied. Although there are arguable grounds to award sanctions, the court exercises its discretion not to do so. Order (one for both motions) signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Against Pltf
Off Calendar Feb-01-2012 Text Ruling
